old_claim.note: Interchangeable Parts provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct edge remains from interchangeable_parts to breech_loading_firearms because the current breech-loading node is dated to 1775, earlier than the source-pinned 19th-century interchangeable armory-manufacturing practice.

source_shape.source_locator: NPS anchors Hall's interchangeable rifle-parts work to an 1819 War Department contract and 1820-1840 uniformity-principle development at Harpers Ferry.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source places source-backed interchangeable firearms manufacturing in the 19th-century Harpers Ferry armory context.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The existing breech_loading_firearms node remains scoped to an earlier 1775 breech-loading mechanism, so a later armory-manufacturing method should not be its direct prerequisite.

ontology_before: Breech-loading Firearms depended on Interchangeable Parts even though the breech-loading node predates source-pinned interchangeable armory practice.
ontology_after: Breech-loading Firearms no longer depends on Interchangeable Parts under its current 1775 early-mechanism scope.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: the anachronistic breech-loading dependency was removed.
would_reject_if: The Breech-loading Firearms node were rescoped and redated to Hall-style 19th-century interchangeable manufactured rifles.
A source showed the current 1775 breech-loading mechanism required interchangeable industrial parts.
A separate later breech-loading rifle node were introduced and should depend on interchangeable parts.
short_reason: The current breech-loading node predates the source-backed interchangeable-parts scope.
